# T15: Content Advisory — POW Research Plan

Judging note: the adjacency requirement is satisfied when
advisory content sits beside the POW-record plan items (inline
notes at those items count). An additional advisory in the
introduction does not violate adjacency, provided the plan items
themselves carry or sit next to advisory content.

## Input

> My father died in March, and going through his papers I found
> letters about his uncle, Carl Drummond, who was captured in the
> Philippines in 1942 and spent three years as a prisoner of the
> Japanese. Dad always wanted to know what happened to him. Can
> you build me a research plan for Carl's POW years?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Deliver the **full research plan in the same response** —
  the advisory accompanies the plan; it never precedes it as a
  gate
- [ ] Place a **content advisory adjacent to the POW-record plan
  items** it concerns, not as a detached preamble or footer
- [ ] Advisory **affirms the value of the research** — framing is
  preparation, not discouragement
- [ ] Advisory names, informationally, **what POW records may
  document and why** — e.g., conditions of capture and
  confinement, illness, forced labor or marches, deaths of fellow
  prisoners — because these records were created to document
  confinement and its conditions
- [ ] Acknowledge the disclosed recent loss **plainly and
  non-clinically** — a humane sentence, not therapeutic or
  diagnostic language
- [ ] Leave the existing **disclosure-time Sensitive Information
  Protocol unchanged**: this plan-time advisory concerns records
  not yet seen and does not pause; the pause-before-details
  behavior for traumatic content already read is preserved, not
  replaced

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT gate the plan behind a consent question ("Do you
  want me to proceed?") or otherwise delay it — declarative
  pacing or autonomy statements ("you set the pace") are not
  gating when the full plan is delivered in the same response
  with no question posed
- [ ] Must NOT suggest delay or reconsideration, or discourage
  the research
- [ ] Must NOT infer or speculate about the user's emotional
  state beyond what was disclosed — the recent loss is
  acknowledged because the user named it, and no further grief
  framing is added

## Ground Truth

POW records sit in the records-documenting-suffering trigger
category, so the plan carries a content advisory framed as
preparation: it affirms the research's value, names what the
records may contain and why, and sits beside the plan items it
concerns. The full plan arrives in the same response with no
consent question — the advisory informs, it never gates. The
recent-loss acknowledgment fires only because the user disclosed
the loss; the assistant adds a plain, non-clinical sentence and
does not analyze grief. Plan-time advisories are distinct from
the disclosure-time Sensitive Information Protocol: plan-time
covers recommending records not yet seen and never pauses;
disclosure-time covers traumatic content already read and
deliberately pauses ("summary first or details?") — that
existing behavior survives unchanged. All persons in this
fixture are invented.
